title: pittosporum tubiflorum
intro: Pittosporum tubiflorum, also known as Indian pear or Kooknur, is a plant species native to India and Sri Lanka, belonging to the Pittosporaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The seeds of Pittosporum tubiflorum have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it beneficial for reducing swelling, pain, and promoting wound healing. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the seeds are often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and also used in herbal preparations such as oils and creams, to leverage its medicinal benefits.
